- **`ConfigProvider`** — wraps the app subtree with configuration state
- **`useConfiguration()`** — hook returning `{ config: UserConfiguration, actions: ConfigActions }`

- **Migration** — imports existing `ielts-settings` from the old `AppSettings` storage

message: Task started: Perform Accessibility and UX Review of Configuration UI Components

details.text: Let me also check the SettingsPage integration and check if there are any relevant theme/color variables used.
Now I have a thorough understanding of both components. Let me create the review document.
Created `docs/accessibility-ux-review-configuration.md` with the full audit report.
- **Medium priority:** Tutor buttons lack `focus-visible` styles; helper text/error text contrast depends on unverified CSS variable values; no confirmation on provider deletion.
- **High priority:** Save models are inconsistent between the two forms (Basic auto-saves on each field change; Advanced requires explicit save).
- **High priority:** ToggleSwitch descriptions are not linked via `aria-describedby` — screen reader users hear the label but not the description.
- **High priority:** Error messages lack `aria-live`/`role="alert"` — screen readers may miss dynamic validation feedback.
- **Positives:** Semantic `<section>`/`<h3>` structure, proper `<label>` associations, `role="switch"` on toggles, responsive grid classes.
- **Low priority:** Long form with no collapsible sections; provider names indistinguishable in dropdown when multiple share the same type.
